title: types: Session extensions and capability structs
description:
## What
Update crates/jmap-chat/src/jmap.rs Session struct and add capability structs.

Session new fields (spec §Capability):
- owner_user_id: Option<String> — MUST be present in federation deployments
- owner_login: Option<String>
- owner_endpoints: Option<Vec<Endpoint>>
- Add capability detection helpers: fn websocket_capability(&self) -> Option<&WebSocketCapability>, fn chat_push_capability(&self) -> Option<&ChatPushCapability>

New structs:
- ChatCapability { max_body_bytes: u64, max_attachment_bytes: u64, max_attachments_per_message: u64, max_group_members: u64, max_space_members: u64, max_roles_per_space: u64, max_channels_per_space: u64, max_categories_per_space: u64, supported_body_types: Vec<String>, supports_threads: bool }
- WebSocketCapability { url: String, supports_push: bool } — for 'urn:ietf:params:jmap:websocket'
- ChatWebSocketCapability {} — for 'urn:ietf:params:jmap:chat:websocket' (empty object)
- ChatPushCapability { max_snippet_bytes: u64, supported_urgency_values: Vec<String>, max_messages_per_push: Option<u64> }

The Session.accounts map values should carry accountCapabilities including ChatCapability.
Add fn chat_capability(&self, account_id: &str) -> Option<&ChatCapability>

## Tests
Update session.json fixture to include ownerUserId, ownerLogin, capability objects
Assert: all capability fields parse correctly
Assert: chat_account_id() still works
Assert: chat_capability() returns the ChatCapability for the primary account
close_reason: Implementation and tests already present from prior work
